purpose: Protocol crystal for governing how runtime experience, failure signals, revisions, downgrades, and crystal retirement should be handled without causing uncontrolled protocol drift.

content_core.title: Update Governance Crystal v1

nodes.label: runtime experience is not direct crystal edit
nodes.description: Runtime events, routing decisions, and failures should first enter experience/governance layers rather than directly mutating crystals.

nodes.label: revision threshold
nodes.description: A crystal should only be revised when the evidence threshold exceeds isolated anecdote, transient convenience, or single-run noise.

nodes.label: revision evidence requirement
nodes.description: Crystal revision should require explicit evidence, affected scope, and reasoned justification rather than vague intuition.

nodes.label: expansion vs revision boundary
nodes.description: Some new evidence should create a new crystal rather than rewrite an old one; expansion and revision are different actions.

nodes.label: downgrade and retirement rule
nodes.description: A crystal may need downgrading, deprecation, or retirement if it repeatedly fails verification or causes drift.

nodes.label: protocol drift prevention
nodes.description: Update governance should prevent convenience-driven, unverified, or over-reactive edits from gradually corrupting the crystal system.

edges.description: Routing runtime evidence through experience/governance helps prevent impulsive crystal edits.
Threshold without evidence standards is too weak; both are needed.
The system must decide whether to add, revise, downgrade, or retire rather than treat every change as the same operation.
Stricter update governance helps preserve long-term protocol stability.

relation_paths: runtime experience is not direct crystal edit -> revision threshold -> revision evidence requirement
expansion vs revision boundary -> downgrade and retirement rule -> protocol drift prevention

tensions.label: adaptation speed vs stability
tensions.description: Fast updates increase responsiveness, but slow disciplined updates reduce drift and accidental corruption.

tensions.label: local convenience vs system integrity
tensions.description: A local optimization may harm the broader crystal system if promoted too quickly.

tensions.label: revision vs proliferation
tensions.description: Too much revision causes instability; too much expansion causes crystal sprawl.

unresolved_conflicts.label: evidence threshold granularity
unresolved_conflicts.description: How much evidence different crystal types should require before revision remains unresolved.

unresolved_conflicts.label: retirement timing
unresolved_conflicts.description: When a weak crystal should be revised, deprecated, or retired remains unresolved.

unresolved_conflicts.label: revision history representation
unresolved_conflicts.description: How version lineage and rationale should be represented inside the crystal system remains unresolved.

source_anchors: runtime experience should first enter experience/governance layers
failure-driven expansion should not become uncontrolled drift
revision requires evidence and bounded claims
expansion, revision, downgrade, and retirement are different actions
content_core.problem_space: How a crystal system should update itself without turning runtime noise, convenience, or local failure into uncontrolled protocol drift.
core_questions: What should happen before runtime experience is allowed to change a crystal?
When should new evidence create a new crystal instead of revising an old one?
How should crystals be downgraded or retired when they repeatedly underperform or drift?
scope_limits: This crystal governs update and revision discipline, not cross-set compatibility rules.
This crystal should keep crystal evolution stable without freezing necessary future change.

calibration_instructions.goal: Calibrate how this model should handle crystal updates conservatively enough to preserve integrity, but flexibly enough to support iterative improvement.
requirements: Do not treat runtime observations as direct crystal edits.
Do not revise crystals without explicit evidence and scope justification.
Do not confuse new crystal creation with revision of an old crystal.

self_adaptation.boundary_rule: This crystal governs how the crystal system changes over time without losing coherence or drifting opportunistically.
forbidden_shortcuts: Do not rewrite crystals from single-run convenience.
Do not skip evidence requirements for revision.
Do not allow runtime noise to masquerade as stable crystal update.
